violations.text: Observed old rodent droppings inside and outside food storage room located in the basement. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. PIC to clean areas to monitor for new rodent activity. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, and spoon ladles) were observed with food debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Staff moved can opener and spoons to be washed, rinsed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2025

Liquid eggs was observed held at (58) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/Temp. control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Liquid eggs observed on ice in a container. PIC instructed staff to add more ice to submerge liquid eggs container in ice. Corrected temp. 41 degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2025

Double doors in front and back area of dining area is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of food establishment must be protected against to entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings in food storage room and various areas of basement along wall/parameter. Also dead rodent caught on mousetrap. Establishment shall increase pest control from once a month to 2x a month. Conduct a full assessment of establishment before inspection and clean all areas where rodent droppings are located in order to determine if there is new activity of rodents. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

Salsa, sour cream, mayo, blue cheese, and butter (labels states all required refrigeration) were observed held at 43-45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC moved all items off of ice and into refrigeration unit. Staff instructed to use ice water bath and submerging TCS foods. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of rodent droppings observed throughout basement storage areas and two deam mice observed in basement. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan and shelves in the walk-in cooler were observed with an accumulation of dust and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

The area next to and behind the fryer in the cook line area has an accumulation of grease and other debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024

Gloves were stored on a hook on the side of a prep unit next to the hand wash sink. Water observed to splash onto gloves. Splash guard to be installed or hook to be permanently relocated. PIC moved gloves to another location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2023

Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensil was removed from water. Inspector reviewed proper storage of utensils with PIC. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Scoop was relocated to hook. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023
Tiles in the ice cream area was observed in poor repair. Pooling water was observed where tiles were missing. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023
